author: Pablo de Lara Guarch
message:
cmake: fix Windows NASM object format and DLL naming

NASM produced 32-bit objects. CMake expands
'-f \' into the compile rule at
enable_language() time and its auto-default is win32 on Windows
(CMAKE_SYSTEM_PROCESSOR is 'AMD64', not 'x86_64'), overriding the
intended '-f win64' from CMAKE_ASM_NASM_FLAGS and yielding
'COFF format does not support any special symbol types'. Set
CMAKE_ASM_NASM_OBJECT_FORMAT before enabling the language.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara Guarch
message:
cmake: remove unnecessary ASM language from project declaration

The project() declaration listed 'LANGUAGES C ASM', enabling the GAS
assembler (cc.exe on MinGW) on all platforms including x86_64. On
x86_64, only ASM_NASM is needed for .asm files and is already enabled
via enable_language(ASM_NASM). The GAS assembler is only needed for
aarch64 and riscv64 (handled by the existing enable_language(ASM) calls
in the conditional blocks).

Removing ASM from LANGUAGES avoids cmake configuring an unnecessary
assembler toolchain on Windows x86_64.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara Guarch <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara Guarch
message:

Building with CMake was causing unresolved symbols in SHA512-NI

author: Pablo de Lara
message:
sm3_mb: fix dispatcher if AVX512 is available

AVX2 implementation was picked for CPUs supporting AVX512,
due to not supporting SM3-NI implementations.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara
message:
sha512_mb: fix dispatcher if AVX512 is available

AVX2 implementation was picked for CPUs supporting AVX512,
due to not supporting SHA512-NI implementations.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara
message:
include: use SHA1-NI and SHA256-NI implementations when supported with AVX2

SHA1-NI and SHA256-NI implementations are faster that AVX2 only
implementations, so when SHA1-NI/SHA256-NI instructions are supported,
those implementations are prioritized, unless AVX512 ISA is supported.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara
message:
Check if buffer is NULL when length is non zero for SHAx/SM3/MD5

Buffer pointer was checked only for HASH_UPDATE/HASH_ENTIRE,
since it is always required to pass a non-NULL buffer.
However, for HASH_FIRST and HASH_LAST, even though this buffer
pointer is optional, it must be non-NULL if length is not 0.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara
message:
mh_sha1/sha256/sha1_murmur3: prevent internal buffer overrun

len and partial_block_len are uint32_t values. If partial_block_len !=0
and len is UINT32_MAX (0xFF...), the addition of these two values would
result in a number smaller than IMB_SHAx_BLOCK_SIZE and therefore, the
whole buffer would be copied into partial_block_buffer, which is
IMB_SHAx_BLOCK_SIZE * 2 byte long, causing a buffer overrun.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara Guarch
message:
cmake: suppress CMAKE_REQUIRED_QUIET unused-variable warning

When isa-l-crypto is consumed via FetchContent, the parent project may
pass CMAKE_REQUIRED_QUIET as a cache variable (e.g. because it uses
CMake's check-compile modules).

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara Guarch
message:
md5_mb: proper cast of returned value

Replace direct (ISAL_MD5_HASH_CTX *) casts of submit/flush return
values with an offsetof-based macro ISAL_MD5_JOB_TO_CTX, matching
the fix applied to sha1_mb, sha256_mb, sha512_mb and sm3_mb.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara
message:
cmake: fix library revision from 25 to 26

LIBISAL_CRYPTO_REVISION was 25, producing libisal_crypto.so.2.25.0,
while the project version is 2.26.0.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: Pablo de Lara
message:
cmake: detect NASM SHA512-NI support and define HAVE_AS_KNOWS_SHA512NI

When NASM supports the vsha512rnds2 instruction, define
HAVE_AS_KNOWS_SHA512NI for both the C compiler and NASM assembler so
that the SHA512-NI and SM3-NI implementations (guarded with
%ifdef / #ifdef HAVE_AS_KNOWS_SHA512NI in their source files) are
compiled in. When the NASM version is too old, the flag is omitted
and those implementations are silently excluded, matching Makefile
behaviour.

Signed-off-by: Pablo de Lara <pablo.de.lara.guarch@intel.com>

author: hsshin
message:
Move headers to include/isa-l_crypto/ subdirectory

This enables users to include headers with the standard pattern.

Changes:
- Move all public headers from include/ to include/isa-l_crypto/
- Move internal headers to include/internal/ subdirectory
- Update CMakeLists.txt and cmake modules for new header paths
- Add include/isa-l_crypto to private include paths for internal builds
- test.h and endian_helper.h no longer are copied in system when library is installed

Resolves: #171
Signed-off-by: hsshin <hsshinmail@gmail.com>

author: hsshin
message:
cmake: use CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR for FetchContent compatibility

Replace CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR with CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR throughout
CMake configuration files to enable proper path resolution when
the project is consumed via FetchContent or add_subdirectory().

When used as a subdirectory, CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR points to the parent
project's root, causing incorrect path resolution for include
directories, configure_file inputs, and other paths.

Signed-off-by: hsshin <hsshinmail@gmail.com>
